originalWorkflowJson.name: If Workflow
originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: If Workflow. This workflow processes data and performs automated tasks.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: If Workflow. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

nodes.name: Get tickets updated after last execution
Get user data of Ticket requester
Update External Id in Zendesk for contact
Update Zendesk ticket with External Id

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: hubspot  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Ticket Exists" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get user data of Ticket requester" node (n8n-nodes-base.zendesk) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Add user data" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update existing ticket" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update Zendesk ticket with External Id" node (n8n-nodes-base.zendesk) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get last execution timestamp" node (n8n-nodes-base.functionItem) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get tickets updated after last execution" node (n8n-nodes-base.zendesk) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Set new last execution timestamp" node (n8n-nodes-base.functionItem) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create new Ticket" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update External Id in Zendesk for contact" node (n8n-nodes-base.zendesk) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create or update contact" node (n8n-nodes-base.hubspot) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Only keep needed data".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
